report_name: UK's NCSC Unveils 'SilentGlass' Hardware to Block Cyberattacks via HDMI and DisplayPort

vulnerabilities.name: Display Connection Protocol Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerabilities in computer monitor connection protocols such as HDMI and DisplayPort can be exploited to conduct espionage, network intrusion, or data exfiltration. A compromised monitor could capture video, inject malicious commands via the I2C/DDC bus, or exfiltrate data, bypassing software-based security controls.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC)
identities.description: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre, part of GCHQ, which developed the intellectual property for the SilentGlass hardware device.

identities.name: GCHQ
identities.description: The UK's Government Communications Headquarters, of which the NCSC is a part.

identities.name: Goldilock Labs
identities.description: The commercial company licensed by the NCSC to manufacture and globally distribute the SilentGlass hardware device.
